name: Baseline Schema + Fixtures (PR-F2, handover F-007)

design_notes.nature_of_this_feature: Handover F-007. Add schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json: the machine-readable JSON Schema for the immutable baseline artifact whose PROSE convention feature 034 already shipped in ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md ('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json -- the machine-readable schema for the baseline artifact this protocol requires (added by a later feature in this phase; not yet present at the time this protocol was authored)'). The protocol document (ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md) is the semantic authority; this feature only encodes its declared shape as a validatable schema, plus positive and negative fixtures, plus indexing the new schema in NIZAM.json and schema/README.md -- directly mirroring feature 038's shipped, fully-amended design (schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json + .agent/contracts/038.json rev 3) as its exemplar. Per .agent/feature_list_005.json feature 037 acceptance core: (1) 'Positive fixture validates' -- a complete pinned baseline (all six Sec 3 reference categories present, every item carrying its own revision and timestamp) validates with zero errors; (2) 'Negative fixture fails' -- realized as TWO named negative fixtures matching the handover's own minimum-case list verbatim ('missing repository SHA'; 'baseline mixing states without timestamps'), each independently rejected. This feature does NOT author or amend ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md's prose (034's scope, already shipped), does NOT define the CLI that produces the artifact (041's scope), and does NOT define the finding or preflight-verdict schemas (039's engineering_finding.schema.json / 038's already-shipped preflight_verdict.schema.json).
design_notes.schema_design: Top-level object with SIX required array properties, one per ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 3 reference category, named in snake_case: 'framework_references', 'repository_references', 'dependency_references', 'ci_references', 'planning_references', 'evidence_references' -- each 'minItems':1 (Sec 3: 'Every baseline artifact states, at minimum, one reference entry per category that applies to the execution'; this schema's minimal, defensible design treats all six as always-applicable, a documented non-goal below). Every array's 'items' $ref a single shared $defs/reference_item shape (DRY, mirrors how 038 reused its own conditional pattern once rather than duplicating it six times): reference_item is 'type':'object' with 'required':['revision','timestamp'] -- BOTH typed 'string' with 'minLength':1 -- and 'additionalProperties':true so each category's own descriptive identification fields (e.g. 'repository', 'name', 'path', 'dependency', 'system') remain open extension points per schema/README.md's own stated house convention ('Permits `additionalProperties` on extension points so consumer repositories can extend a shape without breaking validation, while still enforcing the required keys and enums that make an artifact machine-legible'). CORRECTED IN REVISION 1 (per .agent/qa/037-contract-review.json unsupported_claims): the prior revision mis-quoted this phrase with an accidental duplication ('on extension points on extension points'); this is now the verbatim citation. This directly encodes ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 4's Revision and Timestamp Anchoring Rule verbatim: 'Every evidence item in a baseline is anchored to a declared repository revision ... and a timestamp. An evidence item with no declared revision, or no timestamp, is not a valid baseline entry.' The per-item required:['revision','timestamp'] constraint is the schema's SOLE and DELIBERATE mechanism for the 'MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions' rule: because every fact recorded anywhere in the baseline must carry its own explicit revision AND timestamp with no exceptions, no category can ever contain an unanchored fact -- the two named negative fixtures below are both instances of this one mechanism (a missing 'revision' key; a missing 'timestamp' key on a second, later-revision entry for the same repository), not two different schema mechanisms. Two additional optional top-level properties, 'execution_id' and 'captured_at' (both bare strings, unconstrained format), mirror schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json's own 'execution_id'/'generated_at' optional fields for house consistency with the artifact-location convention (protocol Sec 6: '.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/baseline.json'). Top-level 'additionalProperties':true. $schema draft 2020-12, $id, title, description declared per schema/README.md's own stated conventions and 038's exact precedent; exemplars read and matched: schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json (required-array + additionalProperties-open house pattern, $defs reuse) and ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 3/4/6 (the semantic authority for field categories, anchoring rule, and artifact location). REVISED IN REVISION 1 (per .agent/qa/037-contract-review.json issues[2]): the 'all six categories required with minItems:1' claim above was previously backed ONLY by a bare substring-in-serialized-schema-text check (the surviving verification entry now explicitly labelled a weak, schema-text-only sanity check, not a structural proof) and by per-item revision/timestamp probes exercised solely against repository_references -- the evaluator proved live that a 'five-loose' schema variant requiring only repository_references (the other five categories present as typed, but neither required nor minItems-constrained, properties) passes every one of the original content-anchored entries identically. A new instance-level verification entry now loops over all six category names against the real positive fixture, independently proving both (a) each category is individually required (deleting it in turn raises ValidationError) and (b) each category is independently non-empty (minItems:1; setting it to [] in turn raises ValidationError) -- 12 probes total, closing the gap for all five previously-unproven categories without any new fixture files.
design_notes.naming_landmine_preempted: The LITERAL feature_list_005.json acceptance-test one-liner for this feature glob-discovers fixtures via `glob.glob('tools/fixtures/*baseline*')`, filtering `pos=[f for f in ... if 'valid' in f or 'pos' in f]` and `neg=[f for f in ... if 'invalid' in f or 'neg' in f]`, then validates `pos[0]` and expects `neg[0]` to raise. Because the English word 'invalid' CONTAINS 'valid' as a substring, any fixture named with 'invalid' would ALSO satisfy the pos filter's `'valid' in f` clause -- silently contaminating `pos[0]` with a negative fixture if glob ordering ever placed it first, exactly the class of 'bare substring token' landmine this phase's reviews have repeatedly caught (033/038 precedents). PRE-EMPTED, not merely avoided by luck: this feature's two negative fixtures are named using 'neg' (tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_missing_revision.json, tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_mixed_timestamps.json) and DELIBERATELY never use the word 'invalid' anywhere in their filenames, so neither one is ever a member of the `pos` list under any glob ordering. Dry-run proof (scratch-only, repeated 5 consecutive times to rule out ordering flakiness): `pos == ['.../ecosystem_baseline_valid.json']` exactly, `neg` contains exactly the two 'neg_*' filenames, `set(pos) & set(neg)` is empty, and the literal one-liner exits 0 on every repetition.
design_notes.dry_run_proof: Every mechanism below was proven against SCRATCH COPIES ONLY (a disposable working directory outside the repository tree, under the generator's own scratchpad); nothing under schema/, tools/, ecosystem/, or any other repo-tree path was created, edited, or deleted to produce this proof. (1) The scratch schema self-validates as a Draft202012 schema (jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema). (2) The scratch positive fixture (ecosystem_baseline_valid.json: all six categories populated, every item carrying revision+timestamp) validates with zero errors. (3) The scratch negative fixture 1 (repository_references entry with NO 'revision' key -- handover minimum case 1, 'missing repository SHA') is rejected, citing "'revision' is a required property". (4) The scratch negative fixture 2 (repository_references records TWO facts about the SAME repository at two different revisions, the second carrying no 'timestamp' -- handover minimum case 2, 'baseline mixing states without timestamps') is rejected, citing "'timestamp' is a required property". (5) The literal feature_list_005.json glob-based combined pos+neg acceptance-test one-liner was run VERBATIM (only the schema/fixture paths swapped to their scratch-directory locations) and exits 0 -- repeated 5 consecutive times with zero variance, per naming_landmine_preempted above. (6) LOOSE-VS-STRICT PROBE (both directions, mirroring the exact 038-rev0 gap class): a disposable LOOSE schema variant was built by deep-copying the strict scratch schema and deleting $defs.reference_item.required entirely (reference_item still TYPES revision/timestamp as strings but no longer REQUIRES either). Under LOOSE: the positive fixture still validates (expected, no happy-path regression), but BOTH negative fixtures ALSO validate (VALIDATES, VALIDATES) -- proving a schema that merely types revision/timestamp without requiring them is content-completeness-blind and would silently pass both handover minimum cases. Under STRICT (this contract's actual design, unmodified): the positive fixture validates and BOTH negative fixtures are REJECTED. This is the direct, non-fixture-dependent proof that the per-item required:['revision','timestamp'] constraint -- not merely the properties' presence in the schema -- is the load-bearing mechanism. (7) The planned NIZAM.json edit (append 'schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' to both the top-level 'schemas' array and the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array) was applied to a scratch copy of the REAL NIZAM.json and still validates against registry/nizam-index.schema.json; separately confirmed live that neither the real repository's current NIZAM.json NOR its HEAD copy (git show HEAD:NIZAM.json) contains the string 'ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' today, so the planned indexing check is non-vacuous by construction. (8) The planned schema/README.md edit (new Schemas-table row anchored under the existing '## Schemas' heading; frontmatter version 0.5.0 -> 0.6.0; one matching change_log entry) was applied to a scratch copy of the REAL schema/README.md and: the frontmatter still validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json; the change_log entry's version ('0.6.0') matches the bumped frontmatter version; tools/verify_lib.sh's own vlib_section_grep, run UNMODIFIED, correctly reports absent-on-the-real-HEAD-copy and present-on-the-scratch-after-copy (both directions, anti-vacuity); and a direct replica of vlib_version_increased's own parse-and-compare logic (the scratch dir itself is not a git worktree, so the primitive's exact algorithm was replicated in Python against the scratch working-tree file and the real `git show HEAD:schema/README.md` copy) confirms a strict semver increase (0,6,0) > (0,5,0). (9) BOTH the .agent/ immutability guard (two-part invariant, see run_state_and_immutable_artifacts below) and the vlib_scope_guard allow-list (see concurrency_fence below) were dry-run LIVE against the current real repository state (read-only; no files created or modified by running them) and both currently PASS, confirming the guards are correctly shaped for the present tree before any of this feature's own files exist. REVISION 1 ADDITIONS (per .agent/qa/037-contract-review.json): (10) SIX-CATEGORY LOOSE-VS-STRICT PROBE, both directions: a disposable 'five-loose' scratch schema variant was built by deep-copying the strict scratch schema, narrowing its top-level 'required' array to ['repository_references'] only, and stripping 'minItems' from the other five category properties. The new instance-level verification entry's exact loop logic (delete each of the six categories in turn from the real positive fixture; separately set each to [] in turn; assert ValidationError every time) was run against BOTH variants: against STRICT (this contract's actual design), all 12 probes correctly raise ValidationError, so the loop's overall check exits 0 (PASS). Against the FIVE-LOOSE variant, all 10 probes touching the five non-repository categories (missing-category and empty-array, for framework/dependency/ci/planning/evidence_references) INCORRECTLY validate, so the loop's overall check exits 1 (GAPS FOUND, itemized) -- proving the new entry is the load-bearing distinguishing mechanism the evaluator's issues[2] found missing, not a restatement of the already-existing bare substring check. (11) NIZAM.json DUAL-SURFACE PROBE, both directions: two disposable scratch NIZAM.json variants were built from the real file -- Variant A (top-level 'schemas' array only, module 'key_documents' omitted) and Variant B (module 'key_documents' only, top-level 'schemas' omitted). The new top-level-'schemas'-array-specific verification entry (`assert 'schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' in d['schemas']`) correctly PASSES against Variant A and correctly FAILS against Variant B; the pre-existing entry 12 (module key_documents membership) correctly FAILS against Variant A and correctly PASSES against Variant B. Together the two entries fully close the dual-surface gap the evaluator proved live: no single-surface edit (in either direction) can satisfy both entries simultaneously; only a genuinely dual-surface edit does. (12) The FIXED entry (scope guard with 'ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md' added to the allow-list) was re-run LIVE against the current repository state, which now genuinely contains 036's committed-or-in-flight ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md and a modified ecosystem/README.md: the fixed allow-list PASSES; re-running the prior, rejected (revision-0) allow-list form against the same live state was independently reconfirmed to still FAIL (`vlib_scope_guard: out-of-scope changed path: ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md`), corroborating the evaluator's reproduction was accurate and the fix is both necessary and sufficient.
design_notes.concurrency_fence: REVISED IN REVISION 1 (per .agent/qa/037-contract-review.json issues[0]): written HEAD-relative throughout, deliberately not pinned to either feature 035's or 036's status AT THE MOMENT OF DRAFTING, because both are moving targets in this shared working tree and the orchestrator has stated it will commit 036 before this contract's implementation step runs -- the fence must read correctly whether 035/036's surfaces are still untracked working-tree changes or have already been folded into HEAD by a later commit. Feature 035 (Engineering Audit Protocol) is CONFIRMED COMMITTED to HEAD as of this revision (git log: commit 4b01290, 'feat(005): engineering audit protocol (feature 035)') -- ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md and its one-line ecosystem/README.md nav-annotation flip for the 03 row are therefore now part of the baseline this feature diffs against, not a working-tree change at all, for anything already captured in that commit. Feature 036 (Progress Comparison Protocol) has progressed since this contract was first drafted: its contract (.agent/contracts/036.json) is now 'approved' (not 'proposed') and it is IMPLEMENTING or MAY ALREADY BE COMMITTED by the time this contract's own implementation step runs -- ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md already exists on disk today and ecosystem/README.md carries a second, 036-authored nav-annotation flip (currently an uncommitted working-tree modification, alongside .agent/contracts/036.json / .agent/qa/036-contract-review*.json / .agent/evidence/036-* as untracked artifacts). Regardless of whether 036 is committed before or after this feature implements: this feature's scope-guard allow-list (verification entry naming vlib_scope_guard) explicitly tolerates ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md, ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md, and ecosystem/README.md (035's and 036's legitimate concurrent surfaces) SOLELY to avoid a false-positive scope-guard failure in either state -- if 036 is committed first, these paths simply drop out of the `git status --porcelain` diff entirely and the allow-list entries become inert/unused, not incorrect; if 036 is still uncommitted when this feature implements, the allow-list entries are load-bearing and prevent the exact false-FAIL the evaluator reproduced live against entry 24's revision-0 form (which omitted ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md). This feature's own files_create/files_modify below deliberately OMIT all three paths -- this feature does not create, edit, or delete any of them under any circumstance, whether tracked or untracked. This feature's ecosystem-scoped non-goal per-file existence loop enumerates only the FIVE other not-yet-existing ecosystem protocol documents (00, 04, 05, 06, 08) that neither 035 nor 036 nor this feature is authorized to create -- ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md (035, already committed) and ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md (036, in-flight, may already exist or be committed) are both excluded from that loop, mirroring how 038 excluded both of 034's paths even though only one had been created at 038's own contract-drafting time. The byte-identity non-goal check covers only ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md and ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md (033's and 034's already-shipped, unrelated-to-035/036 protocols, both HEAD-anchored via `git diff --quiet HEAD`, so this check is correct regardless of any later commit landing 035/036's own changes into HEAD). The .agent/ immutability guard is adopted DIRECTLY in its final, twice-amended two-part form from schema/README.md's own 038 rev-3/amendment_2 precedent (not the earlier, superseded allow-list form): (a) `git diff --quiet HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts` must be clean, full stop, no exclusions needed -- this is HEAD-relative by construction and remains correct whether 035/036's own qa/contract artifacts are tracked (post-commit, and therefore excluded from this diff entirely since they'd match HEAD) or still untracked; (b) every untracked ('??') addition under .agent/qa or .agent/contracts must match one of the four glob patterns confining it to a legitimately in-flight phase-005 feature (034-045) or its review artifacts -- this already naturally tolerates 035's and 036's untracked artifacts (and this feature's own, once written) without needing per-feature-ID allow-list maintenance, and requires no revision even as 035/036 move between untracked and committed states.
design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts: This is a CONTRACT-PROPOSAL step (status=proposed): no implementation edits, no git commit, and no .agent/run_state.json rewrite happen under this document -- run_state.json is orchestrator-owned and batched centrally, and this generator was explicitly instructed never to touch it. Nothing under schema/, tools/, ecosystem/, or any other repo-tree surface was created, edited, or deleted to produce this proposal or its dry-run proofs; every proof ran against disposable scratch copies outside the repository (or, where a primitive assumes a git worktree, was replicated algorithmically against the real HEAD copy read-only).

files_create.purpose: JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) for the immutable ecosystem-cycle baseline artifact: six required reference-category arrays (framework/repository/dependency/CI/planning/evidence), each item required to declare its own 'revision' and 'timestamp' (ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 3/4).
POSITIVE fixture: a complete pinned baseline -- all six reference categories populated, every item carrying its own revision and timestamp.
NEGATIVE fixture (handover F-007 minimum case 1): a repository_references entry with NO 'revision' key at all (a missing repository SHA) -- must be rejected.

NEGATIVE fixture (handover F-007 minimum case 2): a baseline mixing states without timestamps -- repository_references records two facts about the same repository at two different revisions, the second carrying no 'timestamp' -- must be rejected.

files_modify.purpose: Index the new schema: append 'schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' to both the top-level 'schemas' array and the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array (mirroring every one of the 12 pre-existing schemas, which already appear in both lists).

Add a Schemas-table row for ecosystem_baseline.schema.json; bump frontmatter version 0.5.0 -> 0.6.0 with a matching change_log entry, per the house pattern the 0.4.0 and 0.5.0 entries already established in this same file.

non_goals: No edit to ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md, ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md, ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md, ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md, ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md, ecosystem/05_release_train_coordination.md, ecosystem/06_simplification_review.md, ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md, ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md, or ecosystem/README.md -- protocol prose and module-index navigation are out of this feature's scope entirely (032/033/034/035/036 and successor-phase-deferred protocols own it, per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3); ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md and ecosystem/README.md are additionally feature 035's scope (committed to HEAD, per design_notes.concurrency_fence) and ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md is feature 036's concurrently in-flight scope (approved and implementing, possibly committed by implementation time) -- this feature's non-goal checks deliberately do not sweep any of the three, regardless of whether 035/036's surfaces are tracked or still untracked at any given moment

No creation or edit of schema/engineering_finding.schema.json, schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, or any other schema/*.json file besides schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json -- the finding schema is feature 039's scope and the preflight-verdict schema (038) already shipped; this feature's own schema is deliberately additionalProperties-open at every level so it composes with, rather than pre-empts, either

No creation or edit of tools/ecosystem_preflight.py or any other file under tools/ besides the three new tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_*.json fixtures -- deterministic baseline-capture tooling (the CLI that actually PRODUCES a baseline artifact) is feature 041's scope; this feature only defines the artifact's shape and disposable fixtures for it
No edit to tools/skill.json, tools/SKILL.md, or tools/interface.md -- capability routing is feature 040's scope

No modification of tools/validate.sh or tools/verify_lib.sh -- this feature composes the existing, unmodified vlib_section_grep, vlib_version_increased, and vlib_scope_guard primitives and does not add a new --target routing discriminator for baseline JSON fixtures (that routing extension, if ever wanted, is out of scope here and not required by any acceptance test)

No edit to CHANGELOG.md -- CHANGELOG consolidation for phase 005 is the release feature's (045, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) responsibility per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.1; this feature records its change solely via schema/README.md's own frontmatter change_log, per that file's own 0.4.0/0.5.0 precedent
No edit to docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, or docs/planning/DEBT.md -- no planning-artifact or debt changes are authorized by this feature

No edit to .agent/run_state.json, any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 037.json, any .agent/qa/*.json, or any prior feature_list_00N.json -- those are immutable audit records or owned by the orchestrator

No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md -- tolerated in the scope-guard allow-list solely to avoid a false-positive scope-guard failure, not because this feature is authorized to modify it

No modelling of per-execution category applicability -- all six Sec 3 reference categories are required with minItems:1 unconditionally; a future feature may relax this if a real execution ever needs to omit an inapplicable category, but no acceptance test in this phase requires that flexibility
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- the deliverable is verified in the working tree; committing is a separate, later pipeline action
No execution of any outstanding phase-005 human gate (H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-RISK) -- this feature's operator_gate is false

verification.acceptance_test: F-007/AT: the schema file exists.
verification.command: test -f schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json && echo PASS || { echo "schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json missing"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity master proof for the schema, mirroring the 033/038 fixture-probe precedent): schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json does not exist at HEAD, so every content-anchored check on it below is proven non-vacuous by construction.
verification.command: ! git show HEAD:schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo PASS || { echo "schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json already exists at HEAD -- content checks below would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-007/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): the schema is itself a valid JSON Schema.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json')))" && echo PASS || { echo "schema is not a valid Draft202012 JSON Schema"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (weak, schema-text-only sanity check -- the schema names all six Sec 3 reference categories by name; NOTE per .agent/qa/037-contract-review.json issues[2], this bare substring-in-serialized-schema-text check cannot by itself distinguish 'all six required' from 'all six merely mentioned as optional properties' -- see the NEXT entry for the load-bearing instance-level proof).
verification.command: python3 -c "import json; s=json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json')); d=json.dumps(s); assert all(k in d for k in ['framework_references','repository_references','dependency_references','ci_references','planning_references','evidence_references'])" && echo PASS || { echo "schema is missing one or more of the six baseline reference categories"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDED IN REVISION 1 (per .agent/qa/037-contract-review.json issues[2]/required_fix -- the load-bearing, instance-level proof that ALL SIX Sec 3 reference categories, not merely repository_references, are each top-level-required with minItems:1): using the real positive fixture as the fully-anchored base, loop over all six category names; for each, (a) delete the category entirely from a copy of the base and assert ValidationError (proves top-level 'required'), and (b) set the category to an empty array [] in a copy of the base and assert ValidationError (proves 'minItems':1). 12 independent probes total (6 categories x 2 conditions); any single unexpected validation fails the whole entry and is itemized by name.

ADDITIONAL (direct, non-fixture-dependent proof of the per-item revision/timestamp anchoring rule, ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 4): a minimal instance whose sole repository_references item omits 'revision' is rejected, and a separate minimal instance whose sole repository_references item omits 'timestamp' is rejected -- each independently, not merely as a side effect of some other missing field.
ADDITIONAL (proves the conditional is correctly scoped -- a fully-anchored minimal instance across all six categories validates with zero errors, not merely that malformed instances fail).
F-007/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): a positive baseline fixture validates AND a negative baseline fixture is rejected (glob-discovered).

ADDITIONAL (both handover F-007 named minimum cases independently confirmed rejected by name, not just whichever the glob happens to pick first): the missing-repository-SHA fixture AND the mixed-timestamps fixture each raise ValidationError by name.

ADDITIONAL (proves the fixture-naming convention itself is non-vacuous, per design_notes.naming_landmine_preempted): neither negative fixture's filename contains the substring 'valid' as a false-positive positive-filter match, and the positive fixture's filename contains neither 'invalid' nor 'neg'.
verification.command: python3 -c "import glob; files=glob.glob('tools/fixtures/*baseline*'); pos=[f for f in files if 'valid' in f or 'pos' in f]; neg=[f for f in files if 'invalid' in f or 'neg' in f]; assert pos==['tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_valid.json'], pos; assert set(neg)=={'tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_missing_revision.json','tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_mixed_timestamps.json'}, neg; assert not (set(pos)&set(neg))" && echo PASS || { echo "fixture naming produced a pos/neg glob collision"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-007/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): NIZAM.json indexes the new schema and stays C4-green.
verification.command: grep -q 'ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' NIZAM.json && bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json; test $? -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "NIZAM.json does not index the schema, or --target NIZAM.json failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity for the prior entry, HEAD-anchored per the 038-amendment_1 precedent so it remains true both before AND after an uncommitted implementation): the HEAD copy of NIZAM.json does NOT contain the string 'ecosystem_baseline.schema.json'.
verification.command: ! (git show HEAD:NIZAM.json | grep -q 'ecosystem_baseline.schema.json') && echo PASS || { echo "NIZAM.json already indexes ecosystem_baseline.schema.json AT HEAD -- prior entry would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (both NIZAM.json indexing surfaces, not only the top-level 'schemas' array): the 'schema' module's own 'key_documents' array also names the new schema.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('NIZAM.json')); mod=[m for m in d['modules'] if m['path']=='schema'][0]; assert 'schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' in mod['key_documents']" && echo PASS || { echo "schema module key_documents does not list the new schema"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDED IN REVISION 1 (per .agent/qa/037-contract-review.json issues[1]/required_fix -- mirrors the immediately-prior entry's rigor for the OTHER indexing surface): the top-level NIZAM.json 'schemas' array itself, exactly, names the new schema. Entry 10's `grep -q ... NIZAM.json` is whole-file and blind to which of the two surfaces holds the string, and C4 (tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json) only confirms listed paths resolve on disk with no cross-list parity check -- so, without this entry, an implementation that indexes the schema ONLY in the module's key_documents array (never touching the top-level 'schemas' array) would pass every other entry in this suite identically to a fully dual-surface-compliant implementation. This entry closes that gap directly.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('NIZAM.json')); assert 'schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' in d['schemas']" && echo PASS || { echo "NIZAM.json's top-level 'schemas' array does not list the new schema"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (schema/README.md indexes the new schema, section-anchored per the module's own 'Schemas' table -- not a vacuous whole-file grep): the '## Schemas' section names ecosystem_baseline.schema.json.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep schema/README.md '^## Schemas$' 'ecosystem_baseline\.schema\.json' && echo PASS || { echo "schema/README.md Schemas table missing the new row"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity for the prior entry): the HEAD copy of schema/README.md's '## Schemas' section does NOT yet name ecosystem_baseline.schema.json.
verification.command: t=$(mktemp); git show HEAD:schema/README.md > "${t}"; source tools/verify_lib.sh; if vlib_section_grep "${t}" '^## Schemas$' 'ecosystem_baseline\.schema\.json'; then echo "HEAD copy already names the schema -- prior entry would be vacuous"; rm -f "${t}"; exit 1; else echo PASS; rm -f "${t}"; fi

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (schema/README.md version-bump discipline, NDS Sec 7 / tools/validate.sh C8): the frontmatter version strictly increased over HEAD.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_version_increased schema/README.md && echo PASS || { echo "schema/README.md version did not strictly increase"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL: schema/README.md's frontmatter change_log includes an entry whose version equals the bumped frontmatter version (satisfies C8 without touching CHANGELOG.md, per the non-goal above).
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml; d=open('schema/README.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; fm=yaml.safe_load(d); v=fm['version']; assert any(str(e.get('version'))==v for e in (fm.get('change_log') or []))" && echo PASS || { echo "no matching change_log entry for the bumped version"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-007/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): compliance validator stays green.
verification.command: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "validate.sh SUMMARY regressed from 11 passed, 0 failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, per-file existence loop -- never an ls-based multi-argument check, per contract 033's rev1 false-pass defect and fix): none of the five OTHER not-yet-existing ecosystem protocol documents (00, 04, 05, 06, 08) was created; ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md is excluded (feature 035's legitimate concurrent creation) and ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md is excluded (feature 036's legitimate concurrent scope, see design_notes.concurrency_fence).
verification.command: bad=0; for f in ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md ecosystem/05_release_train_coordination.md ecosystem/06_simplification_review.md ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md; do if [ -e "$f" ]; then echo "out-of-scope ecosystem protocol document created: $f"; bad=1; fi; done; if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md and ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md (033's and 034's already-shipped protocols, unrelated to 035/036) are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md && echo PASS || { echo "an already-shipped ecosystem protocol document was modified out of scope"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): no schema/*.json file besides this feature's own new schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json and its authorized schema/README.md edit was modified.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- schema/ ':(exclude)schema/README.md' && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope schema/*.json file was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- schema/ ':(exclude)schema/README.md'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): no pre-existing file under tools/ (validate.sh, verify_lib.sh, skill.json, interface.md, or any existing fixture) was modified -- this feature only ADDS new untracked fixtures, which git diff never reports.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- tools/ && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope tools/ file was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- tools/; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, and docs/planning/DEBT.md are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- docs/planning/manifest.json docs/planning/ROADMAP.md docs/planning/DEBT.md && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope planning document was modified"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (.agent/ immutability guard, adopted DIRECTLY in its final, twice-amended two-part invariant form per .agent/contracts/038.json amendment_2 -- not the earlier, superseded allow-list form -- so it tolerates parallel drafting on 035/036 without per-feature-ID maintenance): (a) no TRACKED/HISTORICAL .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record was modified (git diff --quiet HEAD, not an allow-list); (b) every UNTRACKED addition under .agent/qa or .agent/contracts is confined to a legitimately in-flight phase-005 feature (034-045) or its review artifacts -- any untracked file matching the already-committed-history range (031-033) or any other name is rejected.
verification.command: bad=0; if ! git diff --quiet HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; then echo "tracked/historical .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record modified:"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; bad=1; fi; while IFS= read -r line; do case "$line" in "?? "*) path="${line:3}"; case "$path" in .agent/contracts/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/contracts/04[0-5]*.json|.agent/qa/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/qa/04[0-5]*.json) ;; *) echo "out-of-scope untracked .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact: $path"; bad=1 ;; esac ;; esac; done < <(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts); if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: FIXED IN REVISION 1 (per .agent/qa/037-contract-review.json issues[0] -- a live, reproduced false-FAIL: the revision-0 allow-list omitted 'ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md', which feature 036 has since legitimately created, so this entry FAILED today even for a fully-compliant implementation of this feature's own scope). Scope guard, untracked-aware, concurrency-tolerant per design_notes.concurrency_fence -- ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md and ecosystem/README.md are 035's scope (now committed to HEAD) and ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md is 036's concurrently in-flight scope (approved/implementing, possibly committed by implementation time); v0.6.0-release-notes.md is a pre-existing untracked artifact this feature does not touch. HEAD-relative in effect: if 035/036's paths are later committed, they simply drop out of the underlying `git status --porcelain` diff and these allow-list entries become inert, not incorrect. Only this feature's own new/modified files, plus the tolerated concurrent/pre-existing paths, changed outside .agent/.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' 'schema/README.md' 'NIZAM.json' 'tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_valid.json' 'tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_missing_revision.json' 'tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_mixed_timestamps.json' 'ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md' 'ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md' 'ecosystem/README.md' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/037-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/037-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/037-verify-26.txt for the twenty-sixth -- the 'verification' array has exactly 26 entries, revised from 24 in revision 1 per .agent/qa/037-contract-review.json, which added two new entries: the six-category missing/empty instance-level loop, and the top-level NIZAM.json 'schemas' array exact-membership check). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

findings: HIGH (verification[23], the .agent/ vlib_scope_guard entry, then index 24): a live, reproduced false-FAIL -- feature 036 progressed from 'proposed'/contract-revising to 'approved'/IMPLEMENTING since this contract's concurrency_fence was authored, and had already created ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md and modified ecosystem/README.md on disk; the allow-list, built on the stale premise that 036 had not yet created that file, omitted it, so a fully-compliant Generator implementation of feature 037 would still see this entry fail through no fault of its own.

HIGH (verification[11]/[13], the NIZAM.json dual-surface indexing entries): the contract's own stated 'BOTH lists' claim was verified for only one of the two indexing surfaces (the module's key_documents array); a scratch NIZAM.json variant adding the schema ONLY to the module's key_documents array (never touching the top-level 'schemas' array) passed every relevant entry (including C4 by inspection), because no entry specifically, exactly checked the top-level 'schemas' array's own membership.

HIGH (verification[3], the six-category substring check): the schema_design's claim that all six Sec 3 reference categories are top-level-required with minItems:1 was backed only by a bare substring-in-serialized-schema-text check, which cannot distinguish 'required' from 'merely present as an optional property'; a scratch 'five-loose' schema variant requiring only repository_references passed every content-anchored entry identically to the fully-compliant design, using this contract's own three named fixtures, since none of those fixtures ever omits an entire non-repository category.
round0_rejection.fixed_in: revision 1 (this document): (1) verification entry (the vlib_scope_guard scope-guard command) now allow-lists 'ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md' alongside the pre-existing 'ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md' and 'ecosystem/README.md' tolerances, and design_notes.concurrency_fence is rewritten HEAD-relative so it reads correctly whether 035/036's surfaces are still untracked working-tree changes or have already been committed by the time this feature implements; re-proven live against the current repository state (the fixed allow-list PASSES, the original rejected form was independently reconfirmed to still FAIL with the exact error the evaluator reproduced). (2) A new verification entry added immediately after the existing module-key_documents-membership entry, exactly asserting `'schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' in d['schemas']` against the top-level array; re-proven live against two disposable single-surface NIZAM.json variants (top-level-only, module-only) that together with the pre-existing module-membership entry now fully distinguish all three cases (dual-surface compliant / top-level-only / module-only). (3) A new verification entry added immediately after the six-category substring check (now explicitly labelled a weak, non-load-bearing sanity check in its own acceptance_test prose), looping over all six category names against the real positive fixture: for each category, deleting it in turn (proves top-level 'required') and setting it to [] in turn (proves minItems:1) -- 12 probes total, added without any new fixture files; re-proven to PASS against the strict, as-designed schema and to FAIL (10 of 12 probes, itemized) against the evaluator's own 'five-loose' adversarial variant, positively distinguishing the two. Also corrected in this revision: a citation-accuracy typo in design_notes.schema_design (an accidentally duplicated phrase quoting schema/README.md's own house convention) flagged as a non-blocking unsupported_claims item. evidence_convention's entry count updated to reflect the two added verification entries (24 -> 26); estimated_lines is unchanged (165) since the two new entries add verification-array content only, not repository-diff lines -- the scoped schema/fixtures/NIZAM.json/schema-README.md deliverables themselves are unchanged from revision 0.
